title: Systems admiral
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The title of '''systems admiral''' was borne within the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] by the commanders of [[systems force]]s. Systems admirals were sometimes known as '''commodores''', although this latter title also had [[Commodore/Legends|other usages]].

Systems admirals were mid-level commanders within the Imperial military hierarchy: on the one hand, the systems forces that they led were subordinate components of the [[Fleet/Legends|Fleets]] commanded by [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Fleet Admirals]], mere fractions of full [[Sector Group/Legends|Sector Groups]]; but the same systems forces were also subdivided into several [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] commanded by their own [[Admiral/Legends|admirals]], each of which was comparable in size to the small fleets that had been maintained at the Sector level before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

Systems forces were not normally assigned to specific volumes of space within the sector; rather, they were built around force pools, groups of ships and personnel geared to a particular mission profile: [[force escort]] was designed to combat piracy, smuggling and low-level insurgency, while [[force technical services]] was charged with front-line maintenance and salvage duties. Elements of a systems force could thus be sent wherever their particular capabilities were required in the Sector, and as a result, systems admirals might find themselves commanding ships and squadrons light-years apart, in dozens of different star-systems - and having to reorganize and reassign these forces in response to ever-shifting demands. Their tasks were made no easier by the fact that [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] access was still often limited to larger command vessels, requiring them to use courier ships to retain contact with their forces.

==Behind the scenes==
The rank of Systems Admiral appears only in the ''Imperial Sourcebook'', although there are occasional references elsewhere to Imperial officers as "Commodores". In this context, it is likely that both "commodore" and "systems admiral" should be interpreted as titles denoting a specific ''sort'' of command, rather than particular ranks in a graded hierarchy, and it is even possible that they could be borne by officers who did not actually hold flag rank: the specific rank equivalent to a New Republic [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] in Imperial service is usually given as [[Commander/Legends|Commander]], while [[Michael Unther]] appears to have served as the "Admiral" of a full [[Sector fleet/Legends|Sector Fleet]] while holding a rank lower than [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]].

==Sources==
*<!-- 1989-10-XX -->''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''
*<!-- 2012-04-03 -->''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''
*<!-- 2014-10-14 -->''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''
